task_completion.description: Verify that an image is opened in an image editing or processing application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image (either a photograph or a synthetic image with geometric shapes) is visible on the canvas of an application.

task_completion.description: Verify that an edge detection filter or tool is selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A menu, dialog box, or tool panel is open showing an edge detection option (e.g., 'Find Edges', 'Edge Detect', 'Sobel', 'Canny') being selected or configured.

task_completion.description: Verify that the filter has been applied to the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image on the canvas updates from its original state to a new state reflecting the applied filter.

task_completion.description: Verify that the boundaries of the subjects in the image are highlighted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The structural outlines, contours, and edges of the objects in the image are distinctly visible and emphasized compared to the original image.

task_completion.description: Verify that the final image has a sketch-like effect.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image resembles a drawing or sketch, typically characterized by prominent lines on a contrasting background (e.g., mostly dark pixels with bright edges, or mostly light pixels with dark edges) rather than a standard continuous-tone photograph.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the graphical user interface of an application to apply the filter.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with application menus, buttons, or dialogs, rather than just running a pre-written script in a terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the agent modified the actual provided image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The base image being edited should match the expected source (a photo or geometric shapes), not a completely different image downloaded from the internet.

integrity.description: Verify the agent didn't just open a pre-existing sketch image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory should show the transition from the original image to the edge-detected image within the workspace, rather than just opening an already-processed file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original image is likely a real photo from Unsplash (1000x667px or 800x533px) or a synthetic fallback with geometric shapes (800x600px). A successful edge detection will show a significant increase in edge intensity (≥50%), an increase in dark pixels (≥20 percentage points), maintained contrast, and substantial pixel changes (≥10% changed pixels). The final image should look like a sketch with highlighted boundaries.
